original_cc_dflash_output: The discussion focused on training neural networks on different languages. PhD D trained on TI-digits (English) and tested on Italian digits, resulting in poor performance. Training on a single language (Italian) with broad data improved performance compared to task data alone. A third test with three languages (including Italian) showed worse results than the single-language test, with a 20% error rate increase. The key was training on task data versus broad data, with the single-language broad data test showing better results than the multi-language test.
remediated_cc_dflash_output: The discussion focused on the effect of training neural networks on different languages. PhD D and Professor B discussed training a network on TI-digits (English) and testing it on Italian digits, which resulted in poor performance. They also tested a network trained on a single language (Italian) on another language (Spanish), which showed improvement. Finally, a network trained on a three-language database (including Italian, Spanish, and another language) performed better than one trained only on task data. The key takeaway was that training on a broader language set improved performance across different languages.
baseline_ar_output: The discussion focused on the effect of training on different languages. PhD D explained that when networks were trained on task data (e.g., TI-digits) and tested on Italian digits, performance dropped by about 10%. When tested on multilingual data (three languages), performance worsened further, increasing error rates by about 20%. Professor B and PhD D discussed the implications of using broad data versus task-specific data for language transfer.
evidence_selected_baseline_output: The discussion focused on the effect of training on different languages. Professor B asked about the impact of training on one language and testing on another. PhD D explained that training on TI-digits and testing on TIMIT training led to a 10% error rate increase. When moving to multilingual data, the error rate increased by an additional 20%, showing a decline in performance across languages.
evidence_selected_cc_dflash_output: The discussion about the effect of training on different languages involved Professor B and PhD D. Professor B mentioned that training on one language and testing on another language resulted in poor performance, as evidenced by a 10% error rate increase. PhD D noted that the model performed worse when transitioning to multilingual data, indicating that training on a single language does not generalize well to other languages. The key point was that training on one language and testing on another leads to significant performance degradation.

question: Which groups did the members discuss to have been impacted heavily?
reference_answer: Members advocated for aboriginals who faced police brutality, students for their disrupted education, indigenous and rural residents for lack of access to the internet and the black lives movement.

I would like to remind members that any petition presented during the meeting of a special committee must have already been certified by the clerk of petitions. Members who are participating in the meeting in person are kindly asked to bring the signed certificate to the office once the petition has been presented. Presenting petitions, Ms. May. Ms. Elizabeth May (SaanichGulf Islands, GP): Mr. Chair, I rise to present two petitions. They both pertain to the protection of our natural world. One is an e-petition, and it relates to the threat to pollinators globally. We know that honey bees and other pollinators are essential to food production. The petitioners note that research from around the world points to a threat to pollinators, particularly from a class of pesticides known as neonicotinoids. Additionally, the meeting highlighted the impact of climate change on Canadians, including calls for a guaranteed annual livable income and action on fossil fuel subsidies.

Additionally, the discussion included the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on communities and the importance of the UN Declaration on Rights of Indigenous Peoples.

The southern resident killer whales were mentioned as a population in decline, requiring action for their protection. Additionally, Indigenous peoples were discussed in the context of the Coastal GasLink project, with calls to halt the project and uphold the UN Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples. The petitioners also emphasized the need for a guaranteed annual livable income for all Canadians.

reference_answer: The project manager put forward that the speech recognition wouldn't work when several persons wanted to change the channel at the same time, and the cost would surely rise if adding the two technologies. In response to that, the user interface designer said they would just cooperate with some research institute and the vocabulary was limited, so it wouldn't cost much. Then the project manager posed the language problem. The user interface designer answered they would have some language adaptations so the product could be used by people from all countries. Finally, there were still problems with the microphone and camera that were necessary for the two technologies.
